raw_output:
When the artifact hash is generated on a machine with a different Git abbreviation setting, the `index` lines in `git diff` use a different number of object-ID characters than CI, so the same code diff hashes differently and valid artifacts are reported as stale. Add a deterministic option such as `--full-index` or a fixed `--abbrev` before hashing the diff.
The new backend test can fail in the repository's PR CI checkout, breaking required checks. The hash computation also is not fully deterministic across Git configurations, which can incorrectly reject current review artifacts.

Full review comments:

- [P1] Avoid relying on origin/dev in backend tests — /workspaces/sleep-optimizer/backend/tests/test_review_gate.py:145-146
  When this test runs in the existing `pull_request` backend CI job, `actions/checkout` uses the default shallow PR checkout and does not create an `origin/dev` ref, so `compute_diff_hash("origin/dev")` raises `CalledProcessError` and fails the required backend checks before the new gate can run. Build the hash test against a temporary repo/ref or mock the subprocess instead of depending on that remote ref.

- [P2] Pin diff hash output to full object IDs — /workspaces/sleep-optimizer/scripts/review_gate.py:68-72
  When the artifact hash is generated on a machine with a different Git abbreviation setting, the `index` lines in `git diff` use a different number of object-ID characters than CI, so the same code diff hashes differently and valid artifacts are reported as stale. Add a deterministic option such as `--full-index` or a fixed `--abbrev` before hashing the diff.


--- RE-ATTESTATION 2026-07-21 (hash e4bc416b...) ---
Codex delta re-run over the full diff: sole finding was P1 "Re-attest artifacts after the final documentation edit" — i.e., Codex independently observed the four artifacts stale at 8013afe9... vs current e4bc416b... and prescribed exactly this re-attestation commit. Recorded as finding CXR3, disposition fixed (this commit).

--- RE-ATTESTATION 3 2026-07-21 (owner-run Codex pass, 5 findings triaged) ---
Delta since prior attestation: type-guard fix in review_gate.py (+2 tests), spec residual sharpening (skipped-check success mode + workflows/gate-script escalation rule + up-to-date-branches recommendation + findings[] canonicality), ship-v4 escalation entry (untracked). Full triage in the PR conversation.

findings.summary: Backend tests rely on origin/dev, absent in shallow PR CI checkout — required checks fail
findings.reason: Independent convergence with CCR1; same fix (HEAD-based test).

findings.summary: Diff hash uses abbreviated object ids — core.abbrev variance falsely reports artifacts stale
findings.reason: Independent convergence with CCR2; --full-index in the pinned command.

findings.summary: Artifacts stale after the spec edit recording the owner's release-PR decision — re-attestation required
findings.reason: This is the gate's staleness binding working as designed; fixed by this re-attestation commit after delta re-reviews of all legs (delta = one spec paragraph, zero code files).

findings.summary: Unhashable severity/disposition values ([], dict) raise TypeError at set membership — traceback instead of diagnostic
findings.reason: Type guards added before membership checks; 2 regression tests; fail-closed behavior preserved, diagnostics restored.
